creator: lcook@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Alan Somers from comment #1)
Typically bugfix updates assuming that there's not a huge disparity from quarterly (which is not the case, we are jumping from 3.2.1 to 3.2.2) are merged back.  Albeit small fixes, I see no reason _not_ to shoehorn the changes back to 2021Q2 (at the very least, we catch the outliers who experience these bugs).  The release notes do not indicate a major behavioural or feature change, so it's a pretty low-risk MFH.  Your call :)
